title: Suppressing and Manipulating Occurrences During Printing
breadcrumbs: Suppressing and Manipulating Occurrences During Printing
text: Suppressing and Manipulating Occurrences During Printing You can determine whether or not Uniface prints an occurrence present in the component by using conditional ProcScript in the getFocus trigger of the entity. You may want to suppress printing when there is no data to print (empty occurrences), or under specified conditions. You can: Define the entity (or named area frame enclosing the entity) with the printing property Suppress Print if Empty selected. This causes the entity to be left unprinted if it contains no data. Test if the entity contains data with the $empty function. If it does not, you can print a break frame instead, for example, to print an information message. Test for other conditions, such as the value of a specific field. Note: It is usually preferable to retrieve the required data rather than have the print process exclude it. Uniface activates the getFocus trigger of the relevant entity just before printing each occurrence in the component. If Proc code in that trigger returns a negative value when printing, Uniface does not print that occurrence. Example: Suppressing Empty Occurrences Use the $empty function in the getFocus trigger of the outer entity CUSTOMER to test whether the inner entity INVOICE is empty and print a break frame if this is the case. The following ProcScript first tests if there are invoices for the current customer and if the inner entity or named area frame has been defined as Suppress Print if Empty . This ProcScript addresses a situation in which the INVOICE entity has been defined as Suppress Print if Empty. If this were not the case, you would have to test with the following Proc statement: if ($empty(INVOICE) = 1) ;if no invoices ; entity : CUSTOMER trigger getFocus if ($empty(INVOICE) = 2) ;if no invoices & frame suppressed NAME.NO_INVS = CUSTNAME ;fill name field in NO_INVS break frame printbreak "NO_INVOICES" ;when empty, print break frame endif end; getFocus Copy Example: Suppressing Retrieved Occurrences Consider the following example: you are printing a component that contains customers and their invoices. There is a one-to-many relationship between the entity CUSTOMER and the entity INVOICE, with the one entity CUSTOMER painted as the outer entity on the form. To print invoices which are larger than $500, code the following in the getFocus trigger of the INVOICE entity: ; entity : INVOICE trigger getFocus if (AMOUNT.INVOICE <= 500) ;if invoice is less than $500 return -1 ;do not print this invoice endif end; getFocus Copy If you want to use a form both interactively and for this sort of conditional printing, you can use the $printing status request function to first test if Uniface is printing. Note: Do not use the remocc statement during printing to remove an unwanted occurrence. It marks an occurrence for deletion from the database. Manipulating Occurrences Between Entities Occurrence manipulation between entities is generally not advisable when printing. As a rule, it is acceptable for ProcScript in the getFocus trigger of an outer entity to manipulate (via a setocc ProcScript statement, for example) the occurrences of an inner entity, because the inner entity occurs physically lower on the page as far as the print process is concerned. It is never acceptable to do this from inner to outer entity, or between two entities painted on the same horizontal level (whether they are related or not). This is because Uniface builds the print file line by line, occurrence by occurrence. If, halfway through building a line in a print file, the remaining part of the line is manipulated, the results will be difficult to predict. As far as Uniface is concerned, the print line has already been formatted after the first successful getFocus on that line. Related Links trigger getFocus (Entity) Trigger of an entity that handles the processing that should occur after an occurrence gets focus. $printing Return a status indicating whether the current component is printing. $empty Return the empty frame status for an entity or named area frame. printbreak Print the specified break frame.
